name: Lapland (Finland)

colors.symbolism: the gules field of the historic Lappish arms

description: A square red flag charged centrally with the elaborate provincial arms of Lapland, depicting a crowned wild-man figure holding a club.
history: Lapland (Lappi) became a region under Finland's 1997 administrative reform; its banner is the heraldic flag of the regional coat of arms, derived from the historic provincial arms of Lapland documented from the seventeenth century.

features.symbolism: the historic provincial arms of Lapland: a wild man (savage) holding a club, a motif documented from the seventeenth century and referring to the imagined inhabitants of the far north.
